title: ACME-sorbitol (ACMEsorb) Fixation and Enzymatic Dissociation of Whole Soft-Bodied Cnidarians
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f98ap","text":"ACME (Acetic-Methanol) maceration is a tissue dissociation and fixation method described by Jordi Solana’s group that preserves both cell morphology and RNA integrity, and is compatible with single-cell transcriptomics (scRNA-seq) (García-Castro et al., 2021). ACME has been tested on various organisms, including freshwater planarians (Dugesia japonica), annelids (Pristina leidyi), snails (Limnaea stagnalis), vertebrate embryos (Mus musculus and Danio rerio), terrestrial arthropods (Parasteatoda tepidariorum embryos and Drosophila melanogaster larvae), and the starlet sea anemone (Nematostella vectensis), which inhabits brackish waters (García-Castro et al., 2021).","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33},{"style":"italic","offset":337,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":366,"length":15},{"style":"italic","offset":392,"length":17},{"style":"italic","offset":432,"length":12},{"style":"italic","offset":449,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":487,"length":25},{"style":"italic","offset":525,"length":23},{"style":"italic","offset":587,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"31s45","text":"However, for marine organisms, this protocol proved suboptimal in our hands, as we observed cells bursting immediately upon contact with the fixative solution—presumably due to the hypo-osmolarity of the fixative relative to seawater. To compensate for the osmolarity and preserve cell integrity during fixation, we replaced the water (or 1× PBS) fraction in the original ACME formulation with a 0.8 M sorbitol solution (Najle et al., 2023).","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ci9t2","text":"This modified version, which we named ACMEsorb, was successfully applied for single-cell atlasing of various marine species. ACMEsorb fixation of whole organisms, followed by cell dissociation, is the preferred method, as it minimizes cellular stress and reduces viability biases observed during the dissociation of fresh samples.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":38,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ofru","text":"Dissociation post-fixation can be performed either enzymatically, as described here, or mechanically (a protocol using mechanical dissociation after ACMEsorb fixation is available [here]). In this protocol, we use cold-protease to dissociate whole specimens of the starlet sea anemone N. vectensis, after a short fixation with ACMEsorb.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":285,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cl002","text":"Use ultrapure reagents (free of nucleases) and keep your pipettes and workbench clean and free of RNases (clean surfaces with RNase Zap or similar) throughout the whole experiment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"78eln","text":"Use nuclease-free water (for example, Sigma-Aldrich #W4502-1L) for all the solutions and buffers. During sample prep, keep all the reagents and samples on ice unless otherwise indicated in the protocols. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"955ln","text":"ACMEsorb-fixed cells are very resistant to mechanical stress. This is the reason for the high centrifugation speed (1500 x g) used after fixation, which is intended to maximize cell recovery. However, this speed can be optimized (reduced) to avoid cell clumping, provided this is observed after thoroughly resuspending the pellet.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"8jup0","text":"Prepare an appropriate volume of ACMEsorb solution and Resuspension Buffer 1 (RB1) based on the number of samples to be processed. Refer to the 'Materials' section for the composition of each solution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":33,"length":17},{"style":"bold","offset":54,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bkdit","text":"Volumes of fixative solution and buffers are orientative and should be adjusted according to the amount of material. As a reference, the volumes specified here were used for 3 N. vectensis specimens of ~1cm each.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":176,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

mL of 1M Tris-HCl pH8 (Life Technologies #15568-025)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c98jc","text":"Add Milli-Q water to approximatelly 800 mL Buffer 1 (RB1)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"17ui4","text":"Prepare 1X calcium- and magnesium-free PBS (Ca-Mg-free PBS) as the buffer base.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ear8j","text":"Add 10% BSA (Nuclease-free) to a final concentration of 0.1%. For example, add 100 µL per 1 mL of final volume.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dp9vg","text":"Add sorbitol to a final concentration of 0.8 M. For example, if using a 2.4 M sorbitol stock, add 333 µL per 1 mL of final volume.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ag3e3","text":"Add RiboLock RNase inhibitor (Thermo Fisher #EO0382) to a final concentration of 0.2 U/µL (e.g., 5 µL of RiboLock per 1 mL of buffer).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eet6j","text":"Mix gently by inversion or pipetting. Avoid vortexing to preserve enzyme activity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ks5b","text":"Prepare fresh or keep    until use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":22,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"670iu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6i94r","text":"Other reagents:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"15nmc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"css2n","text":"Methanol (Sigma Aldrich #34860-1L-R)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3d6g1","text":"Glacial Acetic Acid (Merck #1000631000)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dnlai","text":"Glycerol (Sigma Aldrich #G7757-1GA)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"17p63","text":"10X Ca-Mg-free PBS (Thermo Fisher #AM9624)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cl92h","text":"1M CaCl2 (Thermo Fisher #J63122.AE)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":7,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b1it6","text":"Cold protease (Sigma-Aldrich #P5380). Dissolve to 125 mg/mL with nuclease-free water, aliquot and freeze at   ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"offset":108,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ftoeq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fguum","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"10m3e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"","unit":32}},"1":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"-20","unit":10}},"2":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"","unit":32}},"3":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"","unit":165}},"4":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"-20","unit":10}}}}
steps: Transfer animal(s) to a small Petri dish or 6-well plate, in a minimum volume of artificial seawater (ASW) at the corresponding salinity depending on the species. Here, we use 14 ppm (1.4 g/L) Red Sea Salts for N. vectensis
Quickly rinse the animals twice with 2 mL Nv-Calcium and Magnesium-free seawater (Nv-CMFSW)
Remove as much Nv-CMFSW as possible, and add 3 mL of freshly made ACMEsorb solution of the following composition: 1950 μl 1.2M sorbitol 300 μl glycerol 300 μl glacial acetic acid 450 μl methanol
Immediately chop the animals into 3-4 mm pieces using a scalpel or a razor blade
Using a wide bore tip or a plastic Pasteur pipette, transfer the tissue pìeces in ACMEsorb to a 5 ml protein LoBind tube
Once fixation is completed, collect the fixed tissue pieces by 1500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes, in a swing-bucket rotor
Remove ( 2.7 mL ) supernatant, leaving ~300 μl in the tube and resuspend the pellet in that volume
Carefully remove as much supernatant as possible and resuspend the pellet with 1 mL RB1 with 1 millimolar (mM) CaCl 2
Add 100 µL DMSO, invert the tube three times to mix and freeze at -80 °C in a Nalgene® Mr Frosty freezing container following the recommendations of the manufacturer.
Gently invert the tube 3 times to mix, and incubate for 00:15:00 at Room temperature on a rotary shaker set at 25 rpm, °C,
Repeat centrifugation at 1500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes, in a swing-bucket rotor
Add 40 µL 125 mg/mL cold protease (Sigma-Aldrich #P5380)
Incubate On ice , pipetting every ~2 minutes, for a total time of 00:15:00
Add 1 mL of Resuspension Buffer 1 (RB1), supplemented with 1 mM CaCl 2
Quench the reaction by adding 1 mL of RB1 supplemented with 10 millimolar (mM) EDTA. Invert twice to mix
Spin down by 1500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes, in a swing-bucket rotor
Carefully remove supernatant and resuspend the pellet with 1 mL RB1
Filter through a 40 μm cell strainer into a new 1.5 mL protein LoBind tube
Count cells using a Neubauer chamber on a 1:100 dilution of the cell suspension stained with 1 µg/µL DAPI
Prepare aliquots of ~1x10 6 cells in 900 µL RB1
creator: Sebastián R. Najle
